_comment: Legal status and SOL tracking. UPDATE these fields as the case progresses. solDeadline is the primary injury date + 2 years (Florida medical malpractice SOL).

defendants.name: Dr. Imran Kayani / Florida Medical Clinic
defendants.allegation: Medical negligence — failure to monitor CK, failure to disclose rhabdo history to pain management

defendants.name: Dr. Michael Rosario-Prieto / BayCare Neurology
defendants.allegation: Medical negligence — 48-day delay in PA submission for urgently needed genetic testing

sol.solBasis: Florida 2-year medical malpractice SOL (Fla. Stat. §95.11(4)(b)). Runs from primary injury (Aug 6, 2025) and discovery (Nov 6, 2025 — Invitae results).
sol.notes: Use the LATER deadline (Nov 6, 2027) to be safe. Pre-suit notice required 90 days before filing.

preSuitNotice.notes: Florida requires pre-suit notice to defendant(s) at least 90 days before filing. Must be sent before the SOL deadline minus 90 days.

counselEngagement.notes: Intake consultation prepared Nov 18, 2025. Actively seeking experienced medical malpractice counsel.
counselEngagement.targetCounselType: Florida medical malpractice — permanent injury, federal employment impact

nextActions.notes: Case is attorney-ready. All records organized.
Consultation Feb 24, 2026. Results will confirm VUS to definitive genetic etiology.

nextActions.action: Send pre-suit notice to both defendants
nextActions.notes: Must be sent at least 90 days before SOL deadline. Requires counsel.

nextActions.action: Request medical records from BayCare — full PA submission log
nextActions.notes: Obtain complete prior authorization submission records to prove 48-day delay.

nextActions.action: Retain expert witness — metabolic medicine / mitochondrial disease
nextActions.notes: Need expert on standard of care for CK monitoring in patients with metabolic history.
Formal present-value calculation of FERS delta, lost earning capacity.

nextActions.action: Compile complete Dr. Kayani chart — all pain management referrals
nextActions.notes: Confirm that Dr. Kayani's referral to Dr. Kamath contained no rhabdo history.
May be needed depending on Mayo Clinic findings and accommodation outcome.
